name: Mc Nix Golf - Roy Nix
about: McNix Golf - Roy Nix is a premier custom golf fitting and club building studio based in Columbus, Georgia, led by Master Clubfitter Roy Nix. The venue specializes in providing highly personalized custom club fitting and building services tailored to a golfer's unique swing, physical attributes, and skill level, with a focus on MOI-matched equipment for superior consistency and performance. While no longer operating a physical retail location, the studio offers a comprehensive online fitting service utilizing a FlightScope launch monitor and an extensive inventory of shafts and head designs, ensuring a professional fitting experience remotely. The service is particularly suited for golfers seeking to lower their handicap, improve shot consistency, and gain a deeper understanding of how properly fitted clubs can enhance their game.
why_golfers_like_it: Specialized MOI-matched custom club fitting for optimized consistency and performance
Online fitting services accessible from home with professional-grade technology
Over 50 years of expertise from a Master Clubfitter and PGA Professional
Personalized approach that can lower handicaps by 4-10 shots per round

food_and_drink.notes: No food or drink services mentioned.

simulator_hardware.notes: The studio utilizes a FlightScope launch monitor for fitting analysis.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: While you can bring your own clubs, the service is specifically designed to fit and build custom golf clubs tailored to your swing, so you won't need pre-existing equipment for the fitting process.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: The venue does not mention food or drink services, and it is a custom fitting studio rather than a social facility, so bringing outside food or drinks is likely not applicable.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulation take?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the duration for an 18-hole simulation, as the primary focus is on custom fitting and club building rather than on-course simulation.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The venue does not specifically mention child-friendly amenities or services, and its focus on professional club fitting suggests it is primarily aimed at adult golfers.
